text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check individual parts of the code, like functions or methods, to ensure they work correctly on their own. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different components work together as expected. A popular approach is test-driven development (TDD), where you write a test before writing the actual code. This method helps you think about requirements and design first. Together, these testing practices give developers confidence. When you see that all tests pass after making changes, you know the software still works correctly, reducing the risk of introducing bugs.
